data.title: CZ: YZiLabs may invest in a time tokenization project on BSC
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CZ published an article on Binance Square to share his thoughts on the KOL time tokenization project.
"Time tokens, that is, KOLs tokenize their own time, have reappeared recently. Although there have been various attempts in the past, they have not yet succeeded. What I have always wanted to do is not time, but coverage. LinkedIn and X both provide this function, but the threshold is too low, resulting in a lot of spam and cannot be used.
This is the idea of ​​a platform that allows serious people to connect with others. The celebrity (KOL) connects his X (or LinkedIn) to the platform to verify their identity. Then, he sets a price for people who send him a message, such as 1 or 0.1 BNB. Anyone can pay a certain amount to send him/her a message. If the KOL does not respond within 5 days, 50% of the payment will be refunded to the requester. The requester still needs to pay, which can prevent spam and encourage them to write messages effectively. KOLs will be incentivized to respond. Their rewards will be more 50%.
I would use a platform like this. I would donate the crypto I receive to charity. This helps filter out the people who are actually willing to contact me. When the other party has something serious to offer, I might move the conversation off the platform, return the payment, or even offer a bounty (for security tips, etc.). I would then be able to open my DMs to everyone and adjust the price over time based on my unpopularity.
This model doesn't solve all problems. Without being arrogant, I think a lot of scammers would be willing to pay 1 BNB to contact me.
YZiLabs might invest in a project that develops a good MVP using BNB/BSC as the main payment option. This should take a few days/week of coding and security review. If the platform is secure enough, I will be the first user. ”

data.title: Stablecoin Market Value Reaches $227.36 Billion With USDT Dominating
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from DefiLlama indicates that the total market value of stablecoins across the network has reached $227.36 billion as of March 8. This marks a 1.03% increase over the past week. USDT holds a significant market share, accounting for 62.98% of the total stablecoin market.
